title: Russia Strikes Odesa with Drones; Worker Killed Near Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant
detail: Russia launched overnight drone attacks on Odesa on April 27, striking residential buildings and a hotel, injuring at least 14 people including children. Separately, a drone strike killed a worker near the Russian-occupied Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP), the first employee killed by a drone attack according to the plant's Russia-installed management. The IAEA's Director General Rafael Grossi warned that 'strikes on or near nuclear power plants can endanger nuclear safety and must not take place.' Russia claimed Ukraine attacked the plant; Ukraine denied targeting it. The ZNPP — Europe's largest nuclear plant — has been under Russian occupation since March 2022 and has suffered multiple incidents threatening nuclear safety. Russian forces also carried out 629 strikes across 45 Zaporizhzhia region settlements that day, damaging homes and civilian infrastructure.

media.caption: Russia strikes Odesa; drone kills worker near Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant — IAEA warns

title: 182 Combat Engagements Across Ukrainian Front on Chernobyl Disaster Anniversary
detail: Ukraine's General Staff reported 182 combat engagements on April 27 — the 40th anniversary of the 1986 Chernobyl nuclear disaster — as Russian forces intensified pressure across multiple front sectors. The Pokrovsk direction was the most intense, with 38 attacks repelled toward Myrnohrad, Pokrovsk, and nearby settlements. The Huliaipole direction saw 22 assault operations in Zaliznychne, Myrne, and Charivne. Russia carried out 43 airstrikes, deployed over 3,600 kamikaze drones, and fired over 1,500 artillery rounds at Ukrainian positions. Ukrainian forces neutralized over 190 UAVs and destroyed Russian armor and personnel in multiple sectors. Over 80 Russian soldiers were killed or wounded in the Pokrovsk direction alone. Euronews reported at least 21 killed across Ukraine and Russia in the mutual exchange of strikes on the Chernobyl anniversary.

media.caption: 182 combat engagements across Ukraine's front lines on April 27 — Chernobyl anniversary
